elements.title: Allocate host resources to the Colima VM
Leaving at least 50% for macOS is recommended
Virtual machine type, architecture, and runtime options
vmtype:vz cputype:host mounttype:virtiofs rosetta:off nestedvirt:off binfmt:on inotify:on autoactivate:on
Run x86 containers at near-native speed
Allows running VMs inside the Colima VM. Enable for Docker-in-Docker, Kubernetes-in-Docker, or testing VM tools. Requires vz VM type. Slight performance overhead.
Name for the VM on your network. Leave empty to use default (colima-default).
Path to a custom qcow2/raw disk image. Leave empty to use Colima's default Ubuntu cloud image.
Registers QEMU emulators for cross-architecture execution. Required for multi-arch Docker builds (buildx) — e.g. building x86 images on ARM. Minimal overhead.
Container runtime and Docker daemon configuration
Configures the Docker daemon inside the VM. Applied to /etc/docker/daemon.json on restart.
Enable k3s cluster inside the VM
Select a k3s release or enter a custom version.
Additional flags passed to k3s server on startup.
Port for the Kubernetes API server. Leave empty for default (6443).
VM networking, DNS, and routing configuration
Assigns a reachable IP address to the VM on your local network. Enables access from other devices on your LAN (e.g. testing from phone). Requires sudo on first use.
Comma-separated. Used by containers for name resolution.
IP address the VM uses to reach the internet. Leave empty for automatic.
Maps host.docker.internal and gateway.docker.internal inside the VM. Enable if your containers need to reach services running on your Mac (e.g. local APIs, databases). Safe to leave on.
Makes the VM's network the preferred route for container traffic. Enable if containers can't reach the internet. Disable if it conflicts with VPN or corporate network — may override your VPN routing.
Share host directories with the VM
How files are shared between your Mac and the VM. Cannot be changed after creation.
Enables filesystem change notifications. Required for hot-reload (webpack, nodemon, vite). Slight CPU overhead from watching files.
Completely disables file sharing between host and VM. Enable if containers are self-contained and don't need access to your local files. Improves VM startup time and reduces resource usage.
Port for SSH access to the VM. Leave empty for auto-assign.

Forwards your Mac's SSH keys into the VM. Enable if you git clone/push from inside containers using SSH. Disable if you don't use SSH keys or have security concerns — VM processes could use your keys.
Adds the VM to your ~/.ssh/config so you can ssh colima directly. Enable for convenience. Disable if you manage SSH config manually or have conflicting host entries.
Shell scripts executed inside the VM on every start
Environment variables passed to the VM on startup
